title: Manually Exchange a Tote by Lane Number
summary: Use the tote management Exchange Lane function to manually trigger a tote exchange by lane number. The training source explicitly states lane-based exchange is supported and provides a formatting example where a lane such as 3050 is entered as 03050.
when_to_use: Use when a manual tote exchange needs to be triggered by lane number through the tote management interface or API-backed Exchange Lane function, and the operator has the lane number to enter in the documented format example.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to exchange by tote ID; this runbook is specific to lane-number entry.
Do not assume lane formats beyond the documented example; the source provides only one example of adding a leading zero.
Do not use this runbook as a complete troubleshooting guide for failed exchange requests; the source does not define full error handling or all valid input formats.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the lane-based manual exchange using the Exchange Lane function and verify the available system response.

safety_notes: The source describes this as a support/operator interface action and does not specify lockout/tagout or production-stop requirements.
Do not invent alternate lane-number formatting rules beyond the documented example from the training source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Exchange Lane function

access_required: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Exchange Lane function
system_preconditions: A lane number is available for the destination or lane to exchange.
The Exchange Lane function is available in the tote management API page shown in the training material.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the tote management API features, including Exchange Lane.

steps.title: Identify the lane number
steps.purpose: Determine the lane value that will be used for the manual exchange request.
steps.instruction: Identify the lane number for the destination or lane to exchange.
steps.expected_result: A lane number is available for entry.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended lane number is known and ready to be formatted for entry.
steps.failure_condition: The lane number is not known or cannot be determined from the available information.

actions.label: Identify lane number
actions.exact_action: Identify the lane number for the destination or lane to exchange.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the lane number cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Exchange Lane — Manually trigger a tote exchange by lane number.

steps.title: Format the lane number with the documented leading zero example
steps.purpose: Enter the lane number in the format shown by the training source.
steps.instruction: Convert the lane number to the documented format by adding a leading zero when needed, such as 3050 to 03050.
steps.expected_result: The lane number is formatted according to the documented example.
steps.healthy_condition: The lane number matches the source example format, such as zero-prefixed 03050 for 3050.
steps.failure_condition: The lane number format is uncertain or the formatted value is not accepted.

actions.label: Format lane number
actions.exact_action: Convert the lane number to the documented format by adding a leading zero when needed, such as 3050 to 03050.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the lane number format is not accepted.
Escalate if the correct lane format cannot be determined beyond the single documented example.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can also do it by lane name, which you have to add a zero to it... so it's zero 3050.
Manual tote exchange is described as available by tote ID or by lane number, with the training giving a lane-format example that adds a leading zero.

steps.title: Open the Exchange Lane function
steps.purpose: Navigate to the tote management function used for lane-based manual exchange.
steps.instruction: Open the Exchange Lane function in the tote management interface or API page.
steps.expected_result: The Exchange Lane function is visible and ready for input.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote management page shows Exchange Lane as an available function.
steps.failure_condition: The Exchange Lane function is not visible or cannot be accessed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: API Features for Tote Management list showing Exchange Lane among the available functions.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Exchange Lane function is not available in the tote management page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Exchange Lane — Manually trigger a tote exchange by lane number.

steps.title: Enter the formatted lane number
steps.purpose: Provide the lane value to the Exchange Lane function.
steps.expected_result: The formatted lane number is populated in the Exchange Lane input.
steps.healthy_condition: The input contains the intended formatted lane number.
steps.failure_condition: The input rejects the lane number or the value cannot be entered as expected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page context where Exchange Lane is available for lane-number entry.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the lane number format is not accepted.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can also do it by lane name, which you have to add a zero to it... so it's zero 3050.
OCR: Exchange Lane — Manually trigger a tote exchange by lane number.

steps.title: Submit the exchange and verify the response
steps.purpose: Trigger the manual exchange and confirm that the lane-based exchange request was accepted or executed based on the available system response.
steps.instruction: Submit the manual exchange action and verify through the available system response that the lane-based exchange was triggered.
steps.expected_result: A manual tote exchange is triggered using the specified lane number in the expected format.
steps.healthy_condition: The available system response indicates the lane-based exchange was triggered.
steps.failure_condition: The exchange does not trigger after entering the formatted lane number, or the response indicates the request was not accepted.

actions.label: Verify response
actions.exact_action: Verify through the available system response that the lane-based exchange was triggered.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page showing Exchange Lane as the function being used for the manual exchange request.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the exchange does not trigger after entering the formatted lane number.
Escalate if the available system response does not confirm the lane-based exchange was triggered.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Exchange Lane — Manually trigger a tote exchange by lane number.
You can manually exchange any tote on the system. OCR: Exchange Tote by tote Id; Exchange Lane by lane number. Transcript example: add a zero, such as 3050 to 03050.

success_criteria: The Exchange Lane function is accessed from the tote management page.
The lane number is entered using the documented format example.
The available system response indicates the lane-based manual exchange was triggered.
healthy_conditions: Exchange Lane is available in the tote management API features.
The lane number is accepted in the documented format example.
The system response confirms the exchange request was triggered.
failure_conditions: The lane number cannot be identified.
The lane number format is not accepted.
The Exchange Lane function is unavailable.
The exchange does not trigger after the formatted lane number is entered.
The source does not define all valid lane-number formats or detailed failure responses.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the lane number format is not accepted.
Escalate if the exchange does not trigger after entering the formatted lane number.
Escalate if the Exchange Lane function is not available in the tote management page.
Escalate when a lane format other than the documented leading-zero example is required, because the source does not define all valid lane formats.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact button label or submission control used to execute Exchange Lane.
The source does not show the exact input field name for the lane number.
The source provides only one lane-formatting example and does not define all valid lane-number formats.
The source does not define the exact success message, error message, or response text for a completed lane exchange.
The source does not specify estimated completion time, production-stop requirements, or LOTO requirements.
